registers.name: CONTROL
registers.description: Control register. Bit 0 ENABLE is stored. Bit 1 SAMPLE_START is a write pulse and reads back as 0. Bit 2 IRQ_ENABLE is stored. Bit 3 ALERT_CLEAR is a write pulse and reads back as 0. Upper bits read as 0.

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: Global enable for periodic sampling behavior.

fields.name: SAMPLE_START
fields.description: Writing 1 requests one conversion and generates a one-cycle sample_req pulse; readback is always 0.

fields.name: ALERT_CLEAR
fields.description: Writing 1 clears alert_status and STATUS.alert_pending; readback is always 0.

registers.name: STATUS
registers.description: Read-only sticky/live status. Bit 0 sample_done is sticky until IRQ_CLEAR bit 1 is written. Bit 1 alert_pending mirrors latched alert_status until ALERT_CLEAR or IRQ_CLEAR bit 0 is written. Bit 2 adc_valid_seen is sticky after first adc_valid until reset. Bit 3 busy is high while a requested sample is pending. Upper bits read as 0.

fields.name: sample_done
fields.description: Sticky indication that at least one adc_valid sample completed since last clear.

fields.name: alert_pending
fields.description: Mirrors latched alert_status until cleared by ALERT_CLEAR or IRQ_CLEAR bit 0.

fields.name: adc_valid_seen
fields.description: Sticky after the first adc_valid after reset.

fields.name: busy
fields.description: High while a requested sample is pending.

registers.name: THRESHOLD
registers.description: Threshold register. threshold_code is bits [11:0]. Upper bits read as 0.

fields.name: threshold_code
fields.description: Temperature threshold compared against filtered temp_code. Alert latches when temp_code is strictly above this value.

registers.name: LATEST_TEMP
registers.description: Latest filtered temperature code. temp_code is bits [11:0]. Upper bits read as 0.

fields.name: temp_code
fields.description: Latest filtered temperature result from the two-sample moving average.

registers.name: SAMPLE_COUNT
registers.description: Completed sample count. Increments on every completed adc_valid sample.

fields.name: sample_count
fields.description: Count of completed adc_valid samples since reset.

registers.name: IRQ_STATUS
registers.description: Read-only sticky interrupt status. Bit 0 alert and bit 1 sample_done. Upper bits read as 0.

registers.name: IRQ_CLEAR
registers.description: Write-one-to-clear register. Writing bit 0 clears IRQ_STATUS.alert and alert_status. Writing bit 1 clears IRQ_STATUS.sample_done and STATUS.sample_done. Reads as 0.

sources.name: ALERT_IRQ
sources.description: Asserted when CONTROL.IRQ_ENABLE is set and any IRQ_STATUS bit is latched. The latched sources are IRQ_STATUS.alert and IRQ_STATUS.sample_done.

init_sequence: Write CONTROL=0 to ensure ENABLE, IRQ_ENABLE, SAMPLE_START, and ALERT_CLEAR are deasserted.
Write THRESHOLD[11:0] with the desired comparison threshold.
If needed, clear stale sticky state by writing IRQ_CLEAR with bit 0 and/or bit 1 set.
Set CONTROL.ENABLE=1 to allow periodic sampling.
Optionally set CONTROL.IRQ_ENABLE=1 to allow alert_irq assertion.
polling_sequence: Poll STATUS.busy to observe pending requested sampling.
Poll STATUS.sample_done or IRQ_STATUS.sample_done to detect completed samples.
Read LATEST_TEMP to obtain the latest filtered temperature code.
Read SAMPLE_COUNT to obtain the completed-sample count.
Read STATUS.alert_pending or IRQ_STATUS.alert to determine whether the measured temperature exceeded THRESHOLD.
irq_sequence: On alert_irq, read IRQ_STATUS and STATUS to determine whether alert and/or sample_done caused the interrupt.
Clear IRQ_STATUS.alert and alert_status by writing IRQ_CLEAR bit 0 = 1.
Clear IRQ_STATUS.sample_done and STATUS.sample_done by writing IRQ_CLEAR bit 1 = 1.
If using CONTROL.ALERT_CLEAR, write bit 3 = 1 to clear alert_status and STATUS.alert_pending.

consistency_notes: All assumptions remain compatible with DIGITAL_SPEC_JSON.
The register map is flat and uses the exact top-level digital ports already specified.
No AXI/APB or additional hierarchy is implied by this register map.
The register addresses are word-addressed at the documented byte offsets 0x00, 0x04, 0x08, 0x0C, 0x10, 0x14, and 0x18.
